key_insight: A 3D thermal-mechanical ABAQUS model shows that 6082 aluminum alloy welds develop the highest temperatures in the weld zone and concentrated residual stress and deformation around the weld seam and heat-affected zone.
engineering_relevance: The study helps predict weld-induced stress and distortion, supporting better process optimization and structural design for aluminum alloy joints.
method: Built a 3D temperature-displacement coupled model in ABAQUS for butt welding joints
Ran transient thermal analysis to compute the welding temperature field
Used the temperature field in mechanical analysis to estimate residual stress and deformation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a clear thermal gradient, stress concentration in the weld/HAZ, and observable deformation near the welding area from the simulation results.
watch_item: The abstract describes a numerical simulation only, so validation against experimental temperature, stress, or distortion measurements is not stated.
paper_title: Thermal-Mechanical Analysis of Residual Stress and Deformation in 6082 Aluminum Alloy Welded Joints
